title: Wrangle 42-95103 Memorial
crew_summary: 8 killed; pilot 1st Lt. Norman J. Hunt; 1 survivor
cause_of_loss: Severe aircraft icing during formation assembly; aircraft spun in and crashed at Wrangle Common
operation_target: Assembling for mission to Saarbrucken, Germany

external_narratives.summary: US War Memorials database entry for the B-24 42-95103 memorial plaque at Wrangle Common, Lincolnshire. The plaque bears the 8th Air Force insignia, a B-24 aircraft depiction, and the 579th Bomb Squadron insignia, commemorating the crew who crashed on 13 July 1944.
key_details: Took off from Wendling airfield assembling for Saarbrucken mission
Severe icing caused aircraft to spin in at Wrangle Common
S/Sgt Mark Osment bailed out and survived
Bombs did not explode but caused anxious moments during ensuing fire

external_narratives.summary: American Air Museum in Britain archive page for the 579th Bomb Squadron, part of the 392nd Bomb Group based at Wendling. Documents the squadron's operations including losses such as the B-24J 42-95103 crash at Wrangle.
key_details: Operated B-24 Liberators in the 8th Air Force

external_narratives.summary: ABMC database listing for the 579th Bomber Squadron, 392nd Bomber Group (Heavy), recording American servicemen buried or memorialised through ABMC cemeteries, including those lost in the Wrangle crash.
key_details: Official US government burial records for 579th Bomb Squadron personnel

external_narratives.summary: Find a Grave memorial for 2nd Lt William J. Hession, navigator of B-24J 42-95103, killed in the Wrangle Common crash on 13 July 1944. Hession was from Illinois and served with the 579th Bomb Squadron, 392nd Bomb Group.
key_details: Navigator 2nd Lt William J. Hession from Illinois confirmed KIA

external_narratives.summary: Find a Grave memorial for T/Sgt Harold C. Wilkinson, radio operator of B-24J 42-95103, killed in the Wrangle Common crash on 13 July 1944. Wilkinson was from West Virginia and served with the 579th Bomb Squadron.
key_details: Radio operator T/Sgt Harold C. Wilkinson from West Virginia confirmed KIA

local_contacts.role: Local authority responsible for memorial upkeep
